title: King Tut Day: Ancient Pharaoh Fame
story: Every November 4, people around the world celebrate King Tut Day to honor the most famous pharaoh in history! King Tutankhamun ruled ancient Egypt over 3,000 years ago, but he became a superstar in modern times when his incredible tomb was discovered filled with golden treasures. Today, museums host special King Tut exhibits, schools teach lessons about ancient Egypt, and kids dress up like pharaohs with golden headpieces and Egyptian costumes. People share photos of mummies, pyramids, and hieroglyphics on social media using hashtags like #KingTutDay. Some families even make Egyptian-themed snacks or try writing their names in hieroglyphics to celebrate this legendary boy king who still fascinates us today!
activity_question: What do people often dress up as on King Tut Day?
bonus_fact: King Tut became pharaoh when he was only 9 years old!

title: Think Smart, Act Smarter Day!
story: Every November 4, people celebrate Use Your Common Sense Day by making smart choices and thinking before they act. Kids might pause before doing something risky, while adults double-check important decisions. Schools sometimes use this day to teach problem-solving skills and logical thinking. It's a perfect reminder that sometimes the best answer is the most obvious one!
activity_question: What do people focus on during Use Your Common Sense Day?
activity_choices: Making smart choices and thinking before acting
Eating only common foods like bread and water
Talking to as many people as possible
activity_answer: Making smart choices and thinking before acting
follow_up_question: What's one situation where you used common sense to solve a problem?
